items.title: Warsh promises inflation will be a 'thing of the past,' cites 'mistake' of prior policy
items.summary: Warsh pledged Tuesday to "get monetary policy right" and defeat the inflation that has bedeviled the central bank for the past five years.

items.title: IBM stock craters 23% after issuing second-quarter earnings warning
items.summary: CEO blamed the shortfall on weakness in the software and infrastructure business because clients shifted money toward hardware purchases.

items.title: Supreme Court Justice Barrett says 'the threat level is really high' in budget testimony
items.summary: Supreme Court Justices Amy Coney Barrett and Elena Kagan are testifying about the high court's budget request, which includes higher spending for security.

items.title: Chamath Palihapitiya says soaring AI token spend will hit companies' earnings
items.summary: Palihapitiya is part of a growing chorus of investors and tech executives who are cautioning that the tokenmaxxing era is coming to an end.

items.title: E. Jean Carroll receives $5.6M from Trump in sex abuse, defamation case: Court filing
Sen. Tim Scott wants to hear from Warsh on data centers and artificial intelligence
items.summary: Sen. Tim Scott, R-S.C., will host Kevin Warsh before the Senate Banking Committee on Wednesday for his first time testifying as Fed chairman.

items.title: Google DeepMind chief Demis Hassabis calls for U.S. to spearhead AI standards body
items.summary: Tech giant's AI boss said "urgent action" was needed as AI capabilities advanced.

items.title: United Airlines' new upsell: Keeping other travelers out of the middle seat
items.summary: United Airlines will allow customers to pay more to keep the middle seat open on its Airbus A321XLRs

items.title: Frontier Airlines to debut in-flight Wi-Fi in 2027 with SpaceX's Starlink
items.summary: Frontier is signing with Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi, with service debuting as early as next year.

items.title: SpaceX alumni building remote-controlled construction equipment land $115 million fund round
items.summary: TerraFirma on Tuesday said it raised $115 to support new hires and the building of a new factory and mission control center.

items.title: Warren Buffett excludes Gates Foundation from his annual donations of Berkshire stock
items.summary: Warren Buffett omitted the Gates Foundation from his annual charitable stock gifts.

items.title: Global shipping industry sounds the alarm over Trump’s Hormuz toll plan
items.summary: For shipping giant Hapag-Lloyd, it is "fundamentally wrong" to charge tolls for passage through international waters, regardless of the country in charge.

items.title: Consumers turn to buy now, pay later for essential expenses — with growing risks
items.summary: Some consumers are using buy now, pay later for essential expenses like groceries, rent and utility bills. But more BNPL users have recently had a late payment.

items.title: Business declares war on Pentagon stock buyback, dividend restrictions moving in Senate
items.summary: The provision was pushed by Sen. Elizabeth Warren, D-Mass., and was included in the National Defense Authorization Act on a bipartisan basis.

items.title: World Cup's biggest spenders show up late as semifinals drive host city travel boom
items.summary: World Cup travel demand to U.S. host cities is spiking as the tournament reaches the semifinal stage.

items.title: Jim Cramer's top 10 things to watch in the stock market Tuesday
items.summary: S&P 500 futures are up after June inflation data came in weaker than expected and earnings season kicked off with the banks.

items.title: Taiwan's second-largest chipmaker starts mass production in Singapore; Citi sees improving outlook
items.summary: Taiwan’s UMC has kicked off mass production of silicon photonics wafers at its Singapore facility amid positive Citi forecast.

items.title: TikTok policy chief defends safety measures amid EU push to limit children's social media access
items.summary: TikTok's policy chief defended the platform's safety measures as the European Union pushes ahead with restricting children's access to social media.

items.title: A French underwear brand is taking on fast fashion — with an IPO
items.summary: Le Slip Français will make its stock market debut in Paris on Tuesday, betting that consumers will pay for locally made clothing amid competition from Shein.

items.title: Retail-trader 'bottleneck bros' eyeing AI supply chain can't wait for SK Hynix options
items.summary: For many of those retail tech traders, SK Hynix's memory business reminds them of Micron – the paragon of the supply-chain bottleneck thesis.

items.title: Jim Cramer says these 'Pavlovian trades' are the stocks that benefit when oil spikes
items.summary: CNBC's Jim Cramer said investors have gravitated toward a familiar group of stocks as oil prices climb.

items.title: Trump to claim declassified intel reveals 2020 election interference: MS NOW
items.summary: Trump said he will make a "Speech to the Nation on Thursday evening, at 9 P.M. Eastern," without providing further details.

items.title: 'Nobody underwrote for that': Private credit faces a key test as higher rates squeeze borrowers
items.summary: Private credit is facing a new pressure point from elevated rates, which could hit under-pressure borrowers.

items.title: Is Hormuz open? Trump's toll threat intensifies rush to bypass the Strait altogether
items.summary: Gulf producers are increasingly relying on alternative routes to keep crude moving as shipping disruptions expose the risks of depending on the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Judge says Trump sued IRS for 'improper purpose'; refers his lawyer to bar
items.summary: Trump's lawsuit against the IRS was settled by the Justice Department with an agreement to create a $1.8 billion "lawfare" fund that has since been scrapped.

items.title: 3 things you need to know about cyclospora
items.summary: Cyclospora, a parasitic infection that can cause weeks of severe diarrhea, is spreading in the U.S. Here's what to know about the disease, and how to stay safe.

items.title: U.S. oil tops $80 after Trump bombs Iran for third day, Tehran attacks tankers in Hormuz
items.summary: Oil prices rose on Tuesday after U.S. President Donald Trump announced plans to impose shipping fees in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Trump administration urges banks to scrutinize lending to immigrants without work authorization
items.summary: Federal bank regulators issued guidance Monday seeking to curb mortgages, auto loans and other consumer credit to immigrants without U.S. work authorization.

items.title: Paramount, WBD hit with lawsuit from 12 states, including California, to block merger
items.summary: A group of states, including California, filed a lawsuit to block the merger between Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery.

items.title: Chipotle is opening its first restaurant in Mexico
items.summary: Fast casual chain Chipotle announced Monday that it is opening its first location in Mexico in the Monterrey area.
